app_caption: **Streamlit v1.57.0** new features `Alert title=` / `:shimmer[]` / `st.bottom` demo — Fuji Speedway / GR Supra / RM Tyre / Dry

error_no_show_fn:
`{version}/app.py` does not define a `show()` function.

Each version's `app.py` must expose `def show():`.
tab1_api_desc:
Streamlit v1.57.0 adds a `title=` parameter to `st.info`, `st.warning`, `st.error`, and `st.success`.

```python
st.success(
    "Personal best!",
    title="🏁 Lap Complete",   # v1.57.0 new
    icon=":material/flag:",   # icon (existing)
)
```

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `title` | Bold header displayed above the alert body |
| body | Alert message (first positional arg, unchanged) |
| `icon` | Material icon or emoji |

> When `title=` is set, the text appears as a bold  
> heading above the alert body.
tab1_preset_info_body: DRS zone in 200m. Drag reduction system available.
tab1_preset_error_body: Sudden RPM drop detected. Check mechanical settings.
tab1_body_placeholder: Front tyre wear is approaching its limit.
tab2_api_desc:
`:shimmer[text]` is a new markdown directive added in v1.57.0.  
It applies an animated shimmer effect to the enclosed text.

```python
st.markdown(":shimmer[Loading telemetry data...]")    # standalone
st.markdown("Analysing :shimmer[12,847 rows] done")   # inline
st.markdown("#### :shimmer[Loading section...]")      # in a heading
```

> **Use case**: Show shimmer placeholders while data is loading,  
> then replace them with real content once ready.  
> Works especially well combined with `st.empty()` for dynamic swaps.
tab2_loading_toggle: Show loading state (watch the shimmer animation)
tab2_shimmer_line2: :shimmer[Fuji Speedway / GR Supra / RM Tyre — fetching session data]
tab2_data_ready_caption: Telemetry data loaded. Toggle "Show loading state" on to see :shimmer[the shimmer animation].
tab2_context_items:
**Context 2 — Inline text:**

Top speed :shimmer[calculating...] km/h ｜ Max RPM :shimmer[aggregating...]

**Context 3 — Caption-style long text:**

:shimmer[Fetching session data — processing 12,847 rows of telemetry]
tab3_api_desc:
`st.bottom` is a new container officially added in v1.57.0.  
Use it with the same context manager syntax as `st.sidebar`  
to pin content to the bottom of the viewport.

```python
with st.bottom():
    st.metric("LAP", "LAP 5")
    st.metric("TIME", "1:39.342")
    # ↑ always pinned to the viewport bottom
```

| Feature | Description |
|---|---|
| Fixed position | Always visible at the bottom of the viewport |
| Any widget | `st.metric`, `st.columns`, buttons, etc. |
| Scroll-independent | Position does not change when the page scrolls |

> **Use cases**: navigation bars, playback controls,  
> status bars, quick-action toolbars.
tab3_what_desc:
`st.bottom` is a container that **pins content to the bottom of the viewport**.  
No matter how much the user scrolls, the content stays at the bottom of the screen —  
making it ideal for controls you always want within reach.

**How it's used in this demo**  
It renders a persistent "Telemetry HUD bar" at the bottom of the page.  
Switch laps in the sidebar and the bottom bar updates in real time.  
No matter which of the three tabs you're viewing, the HUD remains pinned below.
tab3_subheader_note: Implementation Code for This App's st.bottom
tab3_note_desc:
Placed at the end of the `show()` function, *outside* the `st.tabs()` block,  
so it renders regardless of which tab is active.
tab3_code_example:
# At the end of show() — outside the tabs block
with st.bottom:
    st.caption("Telemetry HUD — st.bottom demo")
    b1, b2, b3, b4, b5, b6 = st.columns(6)
    with b1:
        st.metric("LAP",       f"LAP {selected_meta.lap_no}")
    with b2:
        st.metric("TIME",      selected_meta.lap_time_display)
    with b3:
        st.metric("CIRCUIT",   selected_meta.circuit)
    with b4:
        st.metric("MAX SPEED", f"{speed_max:.0f} km/h")
    with b5:
        st.metric("MAX RPM",   f"{rpm_max:.0f}")
    with b6:
        st.metric("TYRE AVG",  f"{tyre_avg:.1f}°C")
bottom_hint: Telemetry HUD — st.bottom demo (stays pinned even while scrolling)
